title: pilosella rothiana subsp. holopolia
intro: Pilosella rothiana subsp. holopolia, also known as a subspecies of the false sowthistle, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have benefits for digestive health and the immune system. In herbal preparations, Pilosella rothiana subsp. holopolia is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to support liver and kidney function, as well as to alleviate symptoms of colds and fever. However, more research is needed to fully confirm the plant's effectiveness and potential side effects, and it should be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
